description: Predator spyware implements a sophisticated error code taxonomy (301-311) that allows operators to diagnose exactly why an implant deployment failed. When an anti-analysis check is triggered, the implant does not simply terminate; it reports a specific error code to the command-and-control infrastructure, then cleans up and exits. This enables operators to distinguish between failures caused by security tools, developer mode, jailbreak detection, geographic restrictions, or multiple instance detection, among others. The system is designed to provide precise feedback, transforming failed deployments into actionable diagnostic events. The non-sequential numbering and missing codes suggest extensibility and shared taxonomy across Intellexa products, allowing for variant-specific or deprecated checks.
name: Error Code Taxonomy for Granular Anti-Analysis Reporting
summary:

When an anti-analysis check is triggered, the implant does not simply terminate; instead, it reports a specific error code to the command-and-control infrastructure, cleans up, and exits.

This system enables operators to distinguish between failures caused by various security tools, developer mode, jailbreak detection, geographic restrictions, or multiple instance detection, among others. The non-sequential numbering and missing codes suggest extensibility and shared taxonomy across Intellexa products, allowing for variant-specific or deprecated checks. 

The attack pattern provides precise feedback, transforming failed deployments into actionable diagnostic events. Its design is intended to transform failed deployments into actionable diagnostic events.

description: Predator uses the getCountNames() function to enumerate all running processes on the device using sysctl and proc_pidpath APIs. It specifically counts how many processes are running from the staging directory (/private/var/tmp/). If two or more instances are detected, the implant reports error code 311 and aborts. This technique is designed to prevent researchers from running multiple analysis instances simultaneously, thereby limiting the ability to perform parallel reverse engineering or sandboxing. By monitoring its own presence, Predator ensures only a single instance operates, reducing the risk of detection and analysis.
name: Multiple Instance Detection via Process Enumeration
summary:
The object contains information about an attack pattern named "Multiple Instance Detection via Process Enumeration". This attack pattern involves a technique used by the Predator malware to prevent researchers from running multiple analysis instances simultaneously. The Predator malware uses the getCountNames() function to enumerate all running processes on the device, specifically counting how many processes are running from the staging directory (/private/var/tmp/). If two or more instances of this process are detected, the implant reports error code 311 and aborts.

This technique is designed to limit the ability to perform parallel reverse engineering or sandboxing, thereby reducing the risk of detection and analysis. By monitoring its own presence, Predator ensures that only a single instance operates, which is essential for the malware's effectiveness. The attack pattern falls under the category of "defense evasion" as it involves evasive actions taken by the Predator malware to evade detection.

The attack pattern is associated with the Miret-Attack kill chain phase and has a sophistication level that is not specified in the available data. It also does not have any external references, goals, or primary motivations mentioned in the provided information.

description: The implant includes a targeted check for iOS Developer Mode, which is commonly enabled by security researchers and developers. The is_developer() function calls sysctlbyname("security.mac.amfi.developer_mode_status") to determine if Developer Mode is active. If detected, Predator aborts execution and reports the event to its operators. This approach allows the spyware to avoid environments likely to be used for analysis or research, reducing the risk of exposure and reverse engineering. By specifically targeting a feature introduced for research purposes, Predator demonstrates an awareness of its threat landscape and adapts its deployment accordingly.

description: Predator employs the is_not_phone() function to detect jailbroken devices by checking for the existence of specific files and directories associated with jailbreak tools and package managers. It uses the access() syscall to look for paths such as /bin/bash, /private/var/tmp/cydia.log, /Applications/Cydia.app, /private/var/lib/apt/, /private/var/lib/cydia, /etc/apt, and /private/var/stash. Additionally, it checks if /bin/ contains more than two entries, which is atypical for stock iOS. If any indicators are found, Predator reports the detection and aborts. This method helps the spyware avoid environments where its behavior could be more easily analyzed or modified.
name: Jailbreak Detection via File and Directory Checks
summary:
The object contains a description of an attack pattern labeled as "Jailbreak Detection via File and Directory Checks". This attack pattern is designed to detect jailbroken devices by employing the is_not_phone() function, which checks for specific files and directories associated with jailbreak tools and package managers. The detection method uses the access() syscall to scan for typical paths such as /bin/bash, /private/var/tmp/cydia.log, /Applications/Cydia.app, and others.

Additionally, it checks if the /bin/ directory contains more than two entries, which is atypical for stock iOS. If any indicators are found, Predator reports the detection and aborts, thereby helping the spyware avoid environments where its behavior could be more easily analyzed or modified. The attack pattern was previously identified in the MITRE ATT&CK framework with technique ID T1516.

This attack pattern falls under the phase "defense-evasion" as it is used to evade detection and analysis of the spyware's behavior.

description: Predator incorporates a geographic restriction mechanism that checks the device's locale settings to avoid executing on devices located in the United States or Israel. If the locale matches either of these jurisdictions, the implant reports error code 309 and refuses to execute. This technique is likely implemented to avoid legal or political repercussions and to reduce the risk of exposure in regions with advanced cybersecurity capabilities. By restricting its operational footprint, Predator minimizes the likelihood of detection by well-resourced organizations and researchers.

description: Predator includes an undocumented crash reporter monitoring system designed to detect if crash logs or crash reporting tools are active on the device.
